Container.Name: OluHome

Branch.TagDescription: Tracking the default branch — auto-updated on every push to main.

Container.Overview:
OluHome is a self-hostable home-inventory and collection-management app. Track items, rooms, photos, valuations, vendors, and acquisitions. Generate PDFs, share read-only links, and (optionally) get AI-assisted item analysis.
This container ships the OluHome web app only. It requires an external PostgreSQL 14+ database — install the official "postgres" or "postgresql" template from Community Applications first, or point DATABASE_URL at any reachable Postgres instance.
On first launch, the container automatically runs database migrations and (if BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_EMAIL and BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD are set) creates an admin user. Subsequent launches are idempotent.
For a fully bundled install (app + Postgres + uploads volume in one stack), use the Docker Compose Manager plugin with the project's docker-compose.prod.yml instead.
Container.Requires: An external PostgreSQL 14+ database. Install one from CA (e.g. linuxserver/postgres, official postgres) before launching, or point DATABASE_URL at any reachable instance.
Container.Description: Self-hostable home-inventory + collection management. See the Overview tab for full setup notes. Required at install: DATABASE_URL, AUTH_SECRET, APP_URL, and the BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_* fields for first-time admin creation.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Host port the web UI is reachable on. Container always listens on 3000.

Config.Name: Uploads
Config.Description: Where item photos, receipts, and other uploads are stored on the array. Back this up alongside your database.

Config.Name: DATABASE_URL
Config.Description: Postgres connection string. Format: postgres://USER:PASSWORD@HOST:PORT/DB. HOST is the Postgres container's name on the docker network or its IP. Append ?sslmode=require if your Postgres requires TLS.

Config.Name: APP_URL
Config.Description: Public URL users reach the app at. MUST include the scheme (http:// or https://) — e.g. https://oluhome.example.com or http://192.168.1.10:3000. Used for NextAuth callbacks and embedded in signed file URLs. If you put OluHome behind a reverse proxy with HTTPS, use the public HTTPS URL here, NOT the internal LAN/Tailscale URL. AUTH_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL are derived from this automatically. The container will refuse to start if the scheme is missing.

Config.Name: AUTH_SECRET
Config.Description: Random 32+ byte secret used to sign session cookies and file-access tokens. Generate with: openssl rand -base64 32. CHANGING THIS LOGS OUT EVERY USER and invalidates outstanding upload/download URLs (existing files are unaffected).

Config.Name: BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_EMAIL
Config.Description: Email for the auto-created admin user. Only used on first boot — if the user already exists, creation is skipped (the password is NOT rotated). Safe to clear after first launch.

Config.Name: BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD
Config.Description: Password for the auto-created admin user. Only used on first boot. Safe to clear after first launch — change the password from inside the app afterwards.

Config.Name: BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_NAME
Config.Description: Display name for the auto-created admin user.

Config.Name: DB_DRIVER
Config.Description: Postgres driver. Leave as 'node-postgres' for self-hosted Postgres. Set to 'neon-serverless' only if pointing DATABASE_URL at Neon's WebSocket endpoint.

Config.Name: STORAGE_DRIVER
Config.Description: File storage backend. 'local' uses the Uploads volume above. 's3' switches to AWS S3 / Linode Object Storage and requires LINODE_* env vars (add them via the Add another Path, Port, Variable, Label or Device button).

Config.Name: STORAGE_LOCAL_DIR
Config.Description: Filesystem path inside the container where uploads are written. Must match the container side of the Uploads volume mapping.

Config.Name: OLUHOME_REGISTRATION_OPEN
Config.Description: When true, the public /register page is enabled so additional users can sign up. Set to false for single-user / household-only installs.

Config.Name: OLUHOME_AI_ENABLED
Config.Description: Enables the AI item-analysis feature globally (identify, condition, provenance, value estimate). Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to be set.

Config.Name: OLUHOME_AI_BETA_ACCESS
Config.Description: Per-user AI access flag. Either OLUHOME_AI_ENABLED or this must be true for any user to access AI features.

Config.Name: OLUHOME_SUBSCRIPTIONS_ENABLED
Config.Description: Enables plan-limit enforcement and the billing UI. Self-hosters typically leave this off — set to false.

Config.Name: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
Config.Description: Anthropic API key for the AI analysis feature. Only needed when OLUHOME_AI_ENABLED=true. Format: sk-ant-...
